text.paragraph:
FNBR 44677540 Date(s): 2/19/24 - 2/19/24
Virginia Mason Issaquah 100 NE Gilman Blvd Issaquah, WA 98027- US (425) 557-8000
Discharge Disposition: *Home (01)

Encounter Type: Clinic -Z Office Visit

Location: DT LIN Allergy
Appointment Type: Allergy New

Location: UV Video Visit
Appointment Type: Ped Behavioral Video Visit New

tr.td:
albuterol (albuterol CFC free 90 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol)
Status: Ordered
Start Date: 9/22/25
2 Inhalation Oral Inhalation Every 4 Hours as needed cough, wheeze, or difficulty breathing; take with spacer. Refills: 0.
Ordering provider: Soong MD, Derrick L

beclomethasone (Qvar Redihaler 40 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol)
Status: Ordered
Start Date: 9/22/25
2 Inhalation Oral Inhalation Twice Daily. rinse mouth and throat after use. Refills: 0.
Ordering provider: Soong MD, Derrick L

Start Date: 8/28/23
1 Each Oral Inhalation One Time. As directed with MDI. Refills: 0.
Ordering provider: Soong MD, Derrick L

item.paragraph:
Soong MD, Derrick L: PERFORM
Event Display: Ped Well Child Check
Authored Date: 20240219160049-0800

Assessment/Plan
_
1. Well child visit Z00.129
Growth and Development: Normal
Immunizations: Up to date
Additional Recommendations: None
Anticipatory Guidance: Standard age appropriate handout given.
Parents separating - going to put the kids in counseling prior to separation to try and help.
Behavior concern - advised therapy is good which they are already organizing, other thing to consider would be the incredible years at the seattle clinic, ok to send referral. Advised that his accepting of new food but not swallowing is likely related to his behavioral concern, and not a functional concern since he does well with other foods.
Follow-up: Routine follow-up 1 year.
2. Asthma J45.909
Ok to try pre-treating with albuterol at school prior to exercise to see if this helps.
Could consider doing ICS if albuterol needed too often.
Orders:
Albuterol (Eqv-ProAir HFA) 90 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol, 2 puff(s), ORAL INH, Q6 Hours, PRN Wheezing, # 2 EA, # Refill (s): 0 # Total Refill (s): 0
Chief Complaint
WCC
History of Present Illness
Concerns:
1. Behavior / Emotions - Teacher has talked about working on appropriate behavior when he is frustrated.
2. Will accept new food, but will not swallow after putting it in his mouth.
NUTRITION: Drinking appropriate amount of milk. No concerns about weight or eating habits. Eats a good variety of foods.
MEDIA: Not greater than 1 hour/day.
ACTIVITIES: Involved in activities that give regular exercise.*
SCHOOL/GRADE: Kindergarten Doing well in school*
Do you have any concerns about how your child is doing in school? NO
Does your child receive any special help in school (e.g., LAP, IEP, etc.)? NO
ELIMINATION: Bowel movements are normal. No daytime accidents. No bedwetting.
SLEEP: Healthy amount of sleep.
DENTITION: Good dental care.
VISION: No vision concerns.
HEARING: No hearing concerns.
SAFETY: No significant issues identified.
*See scanned questionnaire for additional information.
Review of Systems
Pertinent systems reviewed and negative except as per HPI
Physical Exam
Vitals & Measurements
HR: 104(Pulse) BP: 92/48
HT: 112 cm WT: 17.90 kg BMI: 14.27
GEN APPEARANCE: Alert, engaging, pink, no apparent distress. Well developed. Well nourished.
HEENT:
Head: Normocephalic.
Eyes: B/L RR. Conjunctivae pink without discharge. Corneal light reflex symmetric. Extraocular muscles intact. Pupils equal, round, react to light and accommodation. Cover/uncover - test - normal alignment.
Ears: B/L normal canals. B/L tympanic membranes with normal landmarks; no erythema.
Nose: Clear. No discharge.
Mouth/throat: No oral lesions. The pharynx is without exudates or erythema. Dentition healthy.
NECK: Supple. No significant lymphadenopathy.
LUNGS: Clear to auscultation with equal breath sounds. No wheezes, rales or rhonchi.
HEART: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmur. Femoral pulse 2+ and equal.
ABDOMEN: Soft, nontender, normal bowel sounds. No hepatosplenomegaly. No masses.
GENITOURINARY: Normal genitalia. testes descended bilaterally, no hernia, no masses, tanner stage 1
SKIN: No rashes noted.
EXTREMITIES: UE - Normal ROM. LE Normal ROM.
NEUROLOGIC: Normal tone. Cranial nerves grossly intact. Motor/sensory grossly normal.
SPINE: Normal curvature with no defects or dimples.
External Vitals & Measurements
No qualifying data available.
Growth Percentiles
BMI Percentile: 15.44 % (02/19/24 15:25:00)
Height Percentile: 15.33 % (02/19/24 15:25:00)
Weight Percentile: 8.3 % (02/19/24 15:25:00)
Hearing and Vision Screenings
Vision Screen Results
Event Name
Event Result
Date/Time
Eye, Right Visual Acuity
Other: eye doctor
02/19/24 15:25:00

Event Result

02/19/24 15:25:00
Problem List/Past Medical History
Ongoing
Abdominal pain
Asthma
Birth mark Comments: forehead
Chromosomal microduplication
Hemangioma
Prematurity
Strabismus
Underweight in childhood
Historical
No qualifying data
Medications
Albuterol (Eqv-ProAir HFA) 90 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol, 2 puff(s), ORAL INH, Q6 Hours, PRN
Spacer for MDI, 1 EA, ORAL INH, ONCE
Vitamin D, Daily
Allergies
NKA
Social History
Home/Environment
Lives with Father, Mother, Siblings, Grandmother.
Family History
Allergies: Mother and Father.
Anxiety: Mother.
Depression: Mother.
Diabetes mellitus type 2: Sister.
Eczema: Father and Pat. GFather.
Health Maintenance
Pending (in the next year)
There are no current recommendations pending
Satisfied (in the past 1 year)
There are no satisfied recommendations within the defined date range
(Electronically Signed on 02/19/24 04:00 PM)
Soong MD, Derrick L
